examples.title: Pseudocode: Polynomial Regression
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PolynomialRegression
INPUT: X (features), Y (target), degree (polynomial degree)
OUTPUT: coefficients (polynomial coefficients)

BEGIN
 // Transform features to polynomial terms
 X_poly = TRANSFORM_FEATURES(X, degree)
 // Apply standard linear regression
 coefficients = LINEAR_REGRESSION(X_poly, Y)
 RETURN coefficients
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the polynomial regression algorithm. It first transforms the features to polynomial terms using the specified degree, then applies standard linear regression to obtain the polynomial coefficients.

examples.title: Python: Polynomial Regression
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.preprocessing import PolynomialFeatures

# Generate sample data
X = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).reshape(-1, 1)
Y = np.array([2, 4, 6, 8, 10])

# Transform features to polynomial terms
poly_features = PolynomialFeatures(degree=2)
X_poly = poly_features.fit_transform(X)

# Apply standard linear regression
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X_poly, Y)

# Print coefficients
print(model.coef_)
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the polynomial regression implementation. It first generates sample data, then transforms the features to polynomial terms using the specified degree. Finally, it applies standard linear regression to obtain the polynomial coefficients.
